question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the publish() bean submits transactions to the Hedera network using the operator's private key (set via toClient() in TransactionPublisher) without requiring any per-transaction approval from the actual account owners, causing unauthorized fund transfers or state mutations on behalf of accounts the operator does not own?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the publish() reactive flow to submit a CryptoTransfer transaction where senderAccountId is set to an arbitrary third-party account ID (not the operator), causing the operator's single-key signature to be used to authorize a transfer from an account the operator does not control, leading to unauthorized debit of that account?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user abuse the .retry() operator in the publish() Flux pipeline, which retries indefinitely on any error, to cause the monitor to repeatedly re-submit unsigned or improperly signed transactions to the network, bypassing the intended single-submission authorization model and flooding the network with unauthorized transaction attempts?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the onErrorContinue() handler in publish() that silently swallows PublishException errors to mask repeated unauthorized transaction submissions, preventing detection of transactions that were submitted without proper signer approval and allowing the attack to persist undetected?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that publish() calls transactionGenerator.next(0) in a tight generate() loop with no rate-limiting or authorization gate, causing the system to generate and submit an unbounded number of transactions signed by the operator key without any per-transaction owner approval, leading to mass unauthorized transaction submission?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the parallel Flux execution in publish() (using .parallel(publishProperties.getClients())) to concurrently submit multiple transactions signed by the same operator key without requiring individual signer approval for each, effectively multiplying the rate of unauthorized transaction authorization?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any authorization check between transactionGenerator.next() and transactionPublisher::publish in the publish() pipeline to inject a crafted PublishRequest containing a transaction targeting an account they do not own, causing the operator to sign and submit it without owner approval?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the publish() bean uses a single shared operator key (from OperatorProperties with a hardcoded default private key '302e020100300506032b65700422042091132178e72057a1d7528025956fe39b0b847f200ab59b2fdd367017f3087137') to sign all generated transactions, meaning any transaction type submitted through the pipeline is authorized by this single key without requiring approval from the actual account owners?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the ScheduleSignTransactionSupplier invoked through the publish() pipeline, which calls get() to create a ScheduleSignTransaction for any arbitrary scheduleId without verifying that the operator is an authorized signer for that schedule, causing the operator key to sign a scheduled transaction on behalf of an account that never approved it?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the ScheduleCreateTransactionSupplier invoked through publish(), which sets payerAccount to an arbitrary account ID without verifying that the operator controls that account, causing the operator to create a scheduled transaction that designates an uncontrolled account as the fee payer, effectively authorizing fee deduction from a third-party account?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the AccountDeleteTransactionSupplier invoked through publish(), which constructs an AccountDeleteTransaction for any accountId without verifying the operator owns that account, causing the operator key to sign a deletion of an account it does not own, leading to unauthorized account destruction?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the AccountUpdateTransactionSupplier invoked through publish(), which constructs an AccountUpdateTransaction for any accountId and can set a new publicKey without verifying the operator owns that account, causing the operator to sign a key rotation for a third-party account without that account owner's approval?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the CryptoTransferTransactionSupplier invoked through publish(), which sets senderAccountId to any arbitrary account without verifying the operator controls it, causing the operator key to sign a transfer debiting a third-party account without that account owner's signature or approval?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the TokenCreateTransactionSupplier invoked through publish(), which sets treasuryAccountId to an arbitrary account and calls setAutoRenewAccountId() on it without verifying the operator controls that account, causing the operator to sign a token creation that designates a third-party account as treasury without that account's approval?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that publish() uses .flatMapIterable(Function.identity()) to expand a list of PublishRequests without any per-request authorization check, allowing a single generator call to produce multiple transactions targeting different accounts, all signed by the operator without individual owner approvals?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the second onErrorContinue() in publish() that logs unexpected errors but continues the stream, to suppress authorization failures and keep the pipeline running even when transactions are rejected for missing signer signatures, masking the unauthorized authorization attempts?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the .sequential() call after the parallel publish step in publish() to ensure that responses from unauthorized transactions are processed and recorded by mirrorSubscriber::onPublish, causing the system to treat unauthorized transaction submissions as legitimate published events?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the publishMetrics::onSuccess callback in publish() to cause the system to record unauthorized transactions as successful publications, creating a false audit trail that obscures the fact that transactions were submitted without proper signer approval?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the .subscribeOn(Schedulers.single()) in publish() to ensure the entire unauthorized transaction generation and submission pipeline runs on a dedicated thread, preventing any concurrent authorization check from interrupting the flow and guaranteeing continuous unauthorized transaction submission?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the .runOn(Schedulers.newParallel('publisher', publishProperties.getClients())) in publish() to spawn multiple parallel publisher threads that each independently sign and submit transactions using the operator key without any synchronization or authorization gate, amplifying the rate of unauthorized transaction authorization?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the .runOn(Schedulers.newParallel('resolver', publishProperties.getResponseThreads())) in publish() to process up to 40 concurrent transaction responses in parallel, meaning the system can handle and confirm 40 unauthorized transactions simultaneously without any per-transaction owner approval check?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any signature validation in the publish() pipeline before calling transactionPublisher::publish, meaning transactions are submitted to the Hedera network with only the operator's signature even when the transaction type (e.g., AccountDelete, AccountUpdate) requires the target account owner's signature, leading to unauthorized authorization attempts?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the publish() bean is annotated with @ConditionalOnProperty(value = 'hiero.mirror.monitor.publish.enabled', havingValue = 'true', matchIfMissing = true), meaning it is enabled by default without any explicit opt-in, causing the unauthorized transaction authorization pipeline to run automatically without any deliberate activation by an owner?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the mirrorSubscriber::onPublish callback in publish() to cause the subscriber to receive and process responses from unauthorized transactions, potentially triggering downstream actions (e.g., further subscriptions or validations) based on transactions that were never approved by the actual account owners?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the .timeout(properties.getTimeout()) in TransactionPublisher.publish() (called from the publish() pipeline) to cause timed-out unauthorized transactions to be retried via the .retry() operator in publish(), resulting in repeated unauthorized authorization attempts for the same transaction without any owner approval?
